module-5-workshop-schedule-project.label: Module 5 Workshop Schedule Project
module-5-workshop-schedule-project.summary: A community learning center needs one schedule board built from fixed session records, an ordered list, and a fast room lookup.

sketch0.title: Choose a structure for each job

try-workshop-schedule-sketch0.title: Unpack one workshop record
try-workshop-schedule-sketch0.prompt: The starter defines `session = ("Python Basics", "Room A")`. Unpack it into `name` and `room`, then print `Python Basics — Room A`.
try-workshop-schedule-sketch0.hint: Use one unpacking assignment before the f-string.

help.concept: A tuple keeps the two fields together while unpacking gives each position a readable name.
help.hint_1: The left side needs two names.
help.hint_2: Use the supplied tuple instead of typing the output directly.

try-workshop-schedule-sketch0.starterCode:
session = ("Python Basics", "Room A")
# Unpack the tuple and print: Python Basics — Room A
try-workshop-schedule-sketch0.solutionCode:
session = ("Python Basics", "Room A")
name, room = session
print(f"{name} — {room}")

0.message: Unpack session into name and room.

exercise1.title: Create the fixed workshop schedule
exercise1.prompt: Start the workshop schedule project. In step 1 of 3, implement `create_schedule()` so it returns a list containing exactly these tuples in order: `("Python Basics", "Room A")`, `("Data Lab", "Room B")`, and `("Web Starter", "Room C")`.
exercise1.hint: Return the exact three records before adding lookup behavior.

help.concept: A list can hold several fixed tuple records in a predictable order.
help.hint_1: Keep every workshop record as a two-value tuple.
help.hint_2: Run the helper with the sample schedule before moving to the next step.

exercise1.solutionCode:
def create_schedule():
    return [("Python Basics", "Room A"), ("Data Lab", "Room B"), ("Web Starter", "Room C")]

exercise2.title: Build a room lookup
exercise2.prompt: Continue the workshop schedule project. In step 2 of 3, keep `create_schedule()` unchanged. Implement `build_room_lookup(schedule)` so it returns a dictionary mapping each workshop name to its room.
exercise2.hint: Unpack each tuple into a session name and room.

help.concept: A dictionary turns a repeated search by position into a direct lookup by name.
help.hint_1: Use the tuple values as the dictionary key and value.
help.hint_2: Run the helper with the sample schedule before moving to the next step.

exercise3.title: Format the schedule board
exercise3.prompt: Continue the workshop schedule project. In step 3 of 3, keep the schedule and lookup helpers. Implement `format_schedule(schedule)` so it returns a list of strings in the same order, using the format `<session> — <room>` for every tuple.
exercise3.hint: Build a new list in the same order as the schedule.

help.concept: Formatting belongs in a separate helper so the stored records remain reusable.
help.hint_1: Return strings; do not print them inside the helper.
help.hint_2: Run the helper with the sample schedule before moving to the next step.

try_workshop_schedule_sketch0.title: Try it yourself
try_workshop_schedule_sketch0.prompt: The starter defines `session = ("Python Basics", "Room A")`. Unpack it into `name` and `room`, then print `Python Basics — Room A`.

sketch-1.title: Choose a structure for each job
sketch-1.bodyMarkdown:
A community learning center schedules several workshops. Each workshop has a fixed pair of fields: its name and room. The complete schedule needs to preserve order, while staff also need a quick lookup by workshop name.

This project uses a tuple for each fixed record, a list for the ordered schedule, and a dictionary for direct lookup. The final helper formats the same data for a public schedule board without changing how the records are stored.
